title: dioscorea howardiana
intro: Dioscorea howardiana is a species of yam that is native to the Himalayan region, specifically in India and Bhutan. It is a type of vining plant with edible tubers that have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Dioscorea howardiana include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and reduce the risk of certain cancers, as well as its use as a natural remedy for fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Dioscorea howardiana can be prepared as a powder, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
